name: Modal
description: Run Python functions, jobs, web endpoints, and sandboxes on Modal cloud infrastructure.

access.label: Modal CLI/Python SDK
access.detail: Requires a Modal account plus token credentials where the agent runs. BuildingHub records setup metadata only and does not run Modal code.

tools.name: modal
tools.detail: Modal CLI for token inspection, app runs, deployments, and sandbox workflows.

tools.name: MODAL_TOKEN_ID
tools.detail: Modal account token id for automated CLI or SDK access.

tools.name: MODAL_TOKEN_SECRET
tools.detail: Modal account token secret for automated CLI or SDK access.

endpoints.name: Modal docs
endpoints.detail: Canonical Modal documentation for setup and runtime behavior.

endpoints.name: Modal CLI profile
endpoints.detail: Local Modal CLI profile or MODAL_TOKEN_ID/MODAL_TOKEN_SECRET environment variables configured outside BuildingHub.

capabilities.name: modal
capabilities.detail: Modal CLI for token inspection, app runs, deployments, and sandbox workflows.

capabilities.name: MODAL_TOKEN_ID
capabilities.detail: Modal account token id for automated CLI or SDK access.

capabilities.name: MODAL_TOKEN_SECRET
capabilities.detail: Modal account token secret for automated CLI or SDK access.

capabilities.name: MODAL_ENVIRONMENT
capabilities.detail: Optional Modal Environment name when agents should target dev, prod, or another workspace environment.

variables.label: Modal token id
variables.value: MODAL_TOKEN_ID in agent environment or modal token set profile

variables.label: Modal token secret
variables.value: MODAL_TOKEN_SECRET in agent environment or modal token set profile

variables.label: Modal environment
variables.value: MODAL_ENVIRONMENT or default Modal profile environment

variables.label: Budget guardrail
variables.value: allowed GPU classes, runtime, deployment scope, and teardown expectations

steps.title: Install Modal tooling
steps.detail: Make the Modal Python package and modal CLI available in the agent runtime.

steps.title: Configure credentials
steps.detail: Use modal token set or MODAL_TOKEN_ID and MODAL_TOKEN_SECRET in the environment that will run Modal work.

steps.title: Choose environment and budget
steps.detail: Pin the target Modal Environment and write cost, GPU, runtime, and deployment guardrails before agents launch jobs.

steps.title: Install the building
steps.detail: Add Modal to Agent Town after credentials and budget rules are documented.

agentGuide.summary: Use Modal when an agent needs to run Python functions, jobs, web endpoints, or sandbox workloads on Modal after credentials and cost guardrails are approved.
useCases: Run remote Python jobs that need GPUs or serverless scaling.
Deploy or inspect Modal Apps, Functions, web endpoints, Volumes, Secrets, and Sandboxes.
Check whether Modal token credentials or Environment selection are blocking a cloud run.
setup: Read the generated BuildingHub manifest and Modal docs before launching paid cloud workloads.
Run modal token info or modal config show to confirm authentication without printing secrets.
Ask for approval before deploying persistent services, broad parallel jobs, expensive GPUs, or long-running work.
Record Modal app name, environment, command, commit, output paths, and cost-relevant settings in result docs when they matter.

commands.label: Check Modal CLI
commands.detail: Confirms the Modal CLI is installed.

commands.label: Check token profile
commands.detail: Shows the active Modal token profile without exposing the token secret.

commands.label: Show redacted config
commands.detail: Inspects the active Modal configuration with secret redaction enabled by default.

env.name: MODAL_TOKEN_ID
env.detail: Modal token id for automated CLI or SDK access; keep paired with the secret.

env.name: MODAL_TOKEN_SECRET
env.detail: Modal token secret for automated CLI or SDK access; never print it.

env.name: MODAL_ENVIRONMENT
env.detail: Optional target Modal Environment for CLI and SDK operations.
